vllm.title: [Model] Enable FP8 QKV in MoE and refine kernel tuning script
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In this PR, we enable QKV in FP8 for Mixtral models. Gate linear is still in FP16. Since most MoE models now have 8 experts, the shape of gate weights is not divisible by 16, which is required by torch._scaled_mm. However, if we pad gate weights from 8 to 16, then it increases 50% of computation, ma

vllm.title: [Doc] Add checkmark for GPTBigCodeForCausalLM LoRA support
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This was recently added in #3949 but we forgot to update the doc.

vllm.title: [Build] Guard against older CUDA versions when building CUTLASS 3.x kernels
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This checks the CUDA version and uses it to guard against compilation of the CUTLASS 3.x kernels targeted at Sm90 (Hopper) systems for CUDA < 12.0, as a static assert will be triggered in that case. If that happens we fallback to the Ampere kernels.

Thank you for

vllm.title: [CI/Build] CMakeLists: build all extensions' cmake targets at the same time
vllm.author: dtrifiro
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Instead of building one target at a time, we can specify multiple --target flags simultaneously to cmake, this should speed up the build process a little.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Refactor CUTLASS kernels to always take scales that reside on the GPU
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR smooths out the way the kernels handle per tensor vs per token (or per output channel) scales. Previously, the CUTLASS epilogues needed to be passed a float* for the per-token case and a float for the per-tensor case. The code that called the CUTLASS GEMMs handled this by taking torch::tenso
